{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Import Manager",
  "description": "Chi Farms Limited is Nigeria&rsquo;s leading and most integrated poultry company, providing top-quality products and services across the agricultural value chain. As part of the TGI Group, Chi Farms continues to expand its footprint in poultry, aquaculture, and frozen food distribution across Nigeria and West Africa.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position:&nbsp;&nbsp;Import Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Import Manager is responsible for overseeing the financial, regulatory, and operational coordination of import activities following procurement, ensuring timely, compliant, and cost-effective execution of approved imports critical to business operations.
	The role manages trade finance, foreign payments, FX coordination, import documentation, customs clearance, import cost control, and regulatory compliance while partnering closely with Procurement, Treasury, Supply Chain, and external stakeholders to ensure seamless import execution and business continuity.
	The role is responsible for minimizing financial, regulatory, and operational risks associated with imports while providing visibility into import obligations, foreign liabilities, and shipment status to support informed decision-making and uninterrupted operations.


Responsibilities
Import Coordination &amp; Transaction Management:


	Work closely with Procurement to ensure seamless transition from sourcing and supplier award to import execution, trade documentation, shipment coordination, and financial settlement.
	Monitor shipment timelines and cargo movement to ensure timely arrival of imported materials and minimize supply disruptions to operations.
	Track shipment milestones and proactively escalate documentation gaps, shipment delays, regulatory bottlenecks, or financial risks impacting import timelines.
	Coordinate with Supply Chain, Operations, Engineering, Farms, Veterinary Services, Production, and Finance teams to align shipment priorities with business continuity requirements.
	Maintain visibility on open import transactions and ensure timely closure of import cycles.


Trade Finance, FX &amp; Foreign Payment Management:&nbsp;

Investing


	Lead all trade finance and foreign payment activities relating to imports, ensuring timely execution, financial control, and regulatory compliance.
	Manage the administration and processing of trade finance instruments including:
	
		Letters of Credit (LC)
		Form M establishment and management
		Bills for Collection
		Telegraphic Transfers (TT)
		Advance Payments
		Shipping Guarantees
		Bills for Import Collection
	
	
	Serve as the primary liaison with commercial banks, financial institutions, and trade service providers to facilitate foreign exchange sourcing, import financing, and payment execution.
	Ensure compliance with all applicable foreign exchange regulations, central banking guidelines, and trade finance requirements.
	Monitor FX requirements and exposure for import obligations, providing insights to support efficient foreign currency planning and utilization.
	Coordinate timely supplier payment processing to avoid shipment delays, supply interruptions, or contractual penalties.
	Maintain robust tracking of import payment obligations, foreign liabilities, maturity schedules, and outstanding commitments.


Import Documentation, Regulatory &amp; Customs Compliance


	Ensure full compliance with all statutory, customs, and regulatory requirements governing importation activities.
	Oversee the preparation, review, accuracy, and completeness of import-related documentation, including:
	
		Form M
		Proforma Invoice
		Commercial Invoice
		Final Invoice
		Bill of Lading / Airway Bill
		Packing List
		SONCAP Documentation
		NAFDAC Approvals
		Veterinary and Quarantine Permits
		PAAR Processing
		Import Duty Documentation
		Insurance Certificates
		Customs Documentation
	
	
	Manage relationships and engagement with relevant regulatory authorities and trade partners, including:
	
		Nigeria Customs Service
		Federal Ministry of Agriculture
		NAFDAC
		Standards Organisation of Nigeria (SON)
		Quarantine Services
		Port Authorities
		Commercial Banks
		Trade Service Providers
	
	
	Ensure timely processing of customs documentation and cargo clearance while minimizing regulatory exposure, delays, penalties, and non-compliance risks.
	Continuously monitor changes in import regulations, tariffs, levies, and government trade policies to ensure business compliance.


Import Cost Control &amp; Financial Optimization:&nbsp;

Import&amp; Export


	Monitor, review, and control all import-related costs to ensure cost efficiency, transparency, and financial discipline.
	Manage and optimize import-related expenditures including:
	
		Freight costs
		Clearing expenses
		Customs duties and levies
		Port charges
		Insurance costs
		Demurrage and detention costs
	
	
	Drive initiatives to minimize avoidable import-related costs and eliminate inefficiencies in import execution and cargo clearance.
	Conduct landed cost analysis and support Finance and Procurement with financial insights on import economics and cost implications.
	Support budgeting, forecasting, accruals, and financial planning for import-related obligations and foreign payment commitments.
	Ensure timely reconciliation of import costs against approved budgets and shipment documentation.


Stakeholder &amp; Service Provider Management:


	Manage relationships with banks, clearing agents, customs brokers, freight forwarders, shipping lines, and trade service providers to ensure efficient service delivery.
	Monitor clearing and logistics partner performance against agreed service levels, turnaround times, compliance expectations, and cost parameters.
	Resolve escalated issues impacting shipment processing, documentation, customs clearance, or payment execution.
	Collaborate effectively with Procurement, Accounts Payable, Supply Chain, and operational teams to ensure seamless&nbsp;&nbsp;import execution.


Risk Management, Governance &amp; Controls:


	Identify, assess, and mitigate risks associated with import financing and execution, including:
	
		Foreign exchange volatility
		Documentation discrepancies
		Regulatory non-compliance
		Shipment delays
		Customs disputes and penalties
		Supplier payment risks
		Financial exposure arising from import obligations
	
	
	Ensure strong governance, financial controls, and audit readiness across all import transactions and supporting documentation.
	Conduct periodic reconciliation of import records, shipment documentation, payment schedules, and financial obligations to ensure completeness and accuracy.
	Support internal and external audits by maintaining accurate, traceable, and compliant import documentation and records.


Requirements
Qualification:


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor&#39;s Degree&nbsp;


Experience:


	Proven 8-10 years of experience in import management, trade finance, or supply chain/logistics operations
	Hands-on experience managing trade finance instruments &mdash; Letters of Credit (LC), Form M, Bills for Collection, Telegraphic Transfers, Advance Payments, and Shipping Guarantees
	Experience liaising with commercial banks, clearing agents, freight forwarders, and regulatory authorities


Knowledge &amp; Skills:


	Strong knowledge of Nigerian customs regulations, CBN foreign exchange policies, and government trade compliance requirements
	Familiarity with import documentation &mdash; Commercial Invoices, Bills of Lading, SONCAP, PAAR, NAFDAC approvals, Veterinary &amp; Quarantine Permits
	Understanding of landed cost analysis, import cost control, and budget management
	Solid grasp of FX exposure monitoring and foreign payment obligation tracking


Competencies:


	Strong analytical and financial reporting skills
	Excellent stakeholder and relationship management
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy in documentation
	Ability to identify, assess, and mitigate import-related risks
	Strong coordination and communication skills across cross-functional teams
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-29",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-30",

    "@type": "Organization",

Import Manager Job at Chi Farms Limited




        
    

        
        Chi Farms Limited is Nigeria’s leading and most integrated poultry company, providing top-quality products and services across the agricultural value chain. As part of the TGI Group, Chi Farms continues to expand its footprint in poultry, aquaculture, and frozen food distribution across Nigeria and West Africa. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position:  Import Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	The Import Manager is responsible for overseeing the financial, regulatory, and operational coordination of import activities following procurement, ensuring timely, compliant, and cost-effective execution of approved imports critical to business operations.
	The role manages trade finance, foreign payments, FX coordination, import documentation, customs clearance, import cost control, and regulatory compliance while partnering closely with Procurement, Treasury, Supply Chain, and external stakeholders to ensure seamless import execution and business continuity.
	The role is responsible for minimizing financial, regulatory, and operational risks associated with imports while providing visibility into import obligations, foreign liabilities, and shipment status to support informed decision-making and uninterrupted operations.


Responsibilities
Import Coordination & Transaction Management:


	Work closely with Procurement to ensure seamless transition from sourcing and supplier award to import execution, trade documentation, shipment coordination, and financial settlement.
	Monitor shipment timelines and cargo movement to ensure timely arrival of imported materials and minimize supply disruptions to operations.
	Track shipment milestones and proactively escalate documentation gaps, shipment delays, regulatory bottlenecks, or financial risks impacting import timelines.
	Coordinate with Supply Chain, Operations, Engineering, Farms, Veterinary Services, Production, and Finance teams to align shipment priorities with business continuity requirements.
	Maintain visibility on open import transactions and ensure timely closure of import cycles.


Trade Finance, FX & Foreign Payment Management: 

Investing


	Lead all trade finance and foreign payment activities relating to imports, ensuring timely execution, financial control, and regulatory compliance.
	Manage the administration and processing of trade finance instruments including:
	
		Letters of Credit (LC)
		Form M establishment and management
		Bills for Collection
		Telegraphic Transfers (TT)
		Advance Payments
		Shipping Guarantees
		Bills for Import Collection
	
	
	Serve as the primary liaison with commercial banks, financial institutions, and trade service providers to facilitate foreign exchange sourcing, import financing, and payment execution.
	Ensure compliance with all applicable foreign exchange regulations, central banking guidelines, and trade finance requirements.
	Monitor FX requirements and exposure for import obligations, providing insights to support efficient foreign currency planning and utilization.
	Coordinate timely supplier payment processing to avoid shipment delays, supply interruptions, or contractual penalties.
	Maintain robust tracking of import payment obligations, foreign liabilities, maturity schedules, and outstanding commitments.


Import Documentation, Regulatory & Customs Compliance


	Ensure full compliance with all statutory, customs, and regulatory requirements governing importation activities.
	Oversee the preparation, review, accuracy, and completeness of import-related documentation, including:
	
		Form M
		Proforma Invoice
		Commercial Invoice
		Final Invoice
		Bill of Lading / Airway Bill
		Packing List
		SONCAP Documentation
		NAFDAC Approvals
		Veterinary and Quarantine Permits
		PAAR Processing
		Import Duty Documentation
		Insurance Certificates
		Customs Documentation
	
	
	Manage relationships and engagement with relevant regulatory authorities and trade partners, including:
	
		Nigeria Customs Service
		Federal Ministry of Agriculture
		NAFDAC
		Standards Organisation of Nigeria (SON)
		Quarantine Services
		Port Authorities
		Commercial Banks
		Trade Service Providers
	
	
	Ensure timely processing of customs documentation and cargo clearance while minimizing regulatory exposure, delays, penalties, and non-compliance risks.
	Continuously monitor changes in import regulations, tariffs, levies, and government trade policies to ensure business compliance.


Import Cost Control & Financial Optimization: 

Import& Export


	Monitor, review, and control all import-related costs to ensure cost efficiency, transparency, and financial discipline.
	Manage and optimize import-related expenditures including:
	
		Freight costs
		Clearing expenses
		Customs duties and levies
		Port charges
		Insurance costs
		Demurrage and detention costs
	
	
	Drive initiatives to minimize avoidable import-related costs and eliminate inefficiencies in import execution and cargo clearance.
	Conduct landed cost analysis and support Finance and Procurement with financial insights on import economics and cost implications.
	Support budgeting, forecasting, accruals, and financial planning for import-related obligations and foreign payment commitments.
	Ensure timely reconciliation of import costs against approved budgets and shipment documentation.


Stakeholder & Service Provider Management:


	Manage relationships with banks, clearing agents, customs brokers, freight forwarders, shipping lines, and trade service providers to ensure efficient service delivery.
	Monitor clearing and logistics partner performance against agreed service levels, turnaround times, compliance expectations, and cost parameters.
	Resolve escalated issues impacting shipment processing, documentation, customs clearance, or payment execution.
	Collaborate effectively with Procurement, Accounts Payable, Supply Chain, and operational teams to ensure seamless  import execution.


Risk Management, Governance & Controls:


	Identify, assess, and mitigate risks associated with import financing and execution, including:
	
		Foreign exchange volatility
		Documentation discrepancies
		Regulatory non-compliance
		Shipment delays
		Customs disputes and penalties
		Supplier payment risks
		Financial exposure arising from import obligations
	
	
	Ensure strong governance, financial controls, and audit readiness across all import transactions and supporting documentation.
	Conduct periodic reconciliation of import records, shipment documentation, payment schedules, and financial obligations to ensure completeness and accuracy.
	Support internal and external audits by maintaining accurate, traceable, and compliant import documentation and records.


Requirements
Qualification:


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree 


Experience:


	Proven 8-10 years of experience in import management, trade finance, or supply chain/logistics operations
	Hands-on experience managing trade finance instruments — Letters of Credit (LC), Form M, Bills for Collection, Telegraphic Transfers, Advance Payments, and Shipping Guarantees
	Experience liaising with commercial banks, clearing agents, freight forwarders, and regulatory authorities


Knowledge & Skills:


	Strong knowledge of Nigerian customs regulations, CBN foreign exchange policies, and government trade compliance requirements
	Familiarity with import documentation — Commercial Invoices, Bills of Lading, SONCAP, PAAR, NAFDAC approvals, Veterinary & Quarantine Permits
	Understanding of landed cost analysis, import cost control, and budget management
	Solid grasp of FX exposure monitoring and foreign payment obligation tracking


Competencies:


	Strong analytical and financial reporting skills
	Excellent stakeholder and relationship management
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy in documentation
	Ability to identify, assess, and mitigate import-related risks
	Strong coordination and communication skills across cross-functional teams

  "title": "Video / Motion Animator",
  "description": "Feegor is an innovative B2B e-commerce company that enables retailers discover, connect, and source goods directly from manufacturers and major wholesalers nationwide. We are the technology backbone that ensures easy, trusted and efficient transactions in Nigeria&rsquo;s Informal retail sector.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job osition: Video / Motion Animator
Job Location: Lagos

About the Role


	We are looking for a creative and detail-oriented Video / Motion Animator to join our team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for creating engaging visual content through animation, motion graphics, video editing, and storytelling to support our brand, marketing campaigns, product communication, and digital platforms.


Key Responsibilities


	Create engaging motion graphics, animations, and video content for marketing campaigns, social media, product launches, and internal communications.
	Develop creative concepts, storyboards, and visual narratives based on project requirements.
	Edit and enhance videos with strong attention to pacing, transitions, sound, and visual effects.
	Create 2D/3D animations, explainer videos, promotional videos, and branded content.
	Collaborate with designers, marketers, and other teams to translate ideas into compelling visual experiences.
	Maintain consistency with brand guidelines across all video and animation content.
	Stay updated on industry trends, animation styles, and emerging creative tools.
	Manage multiple projects while meeting deadlines and quality expectations.


Requirements


	Proven experience as a Video Animator, Motion Designer, or similar creative role.
	Strong portfolio showcasing motion graphics, animation, and video editing projects.
	Proficiency in tools such as Adobe After Effects, Premiere Pro, Illustrator, Photoshop, or similar software.
	Experience with animation principles, storytelling, typography, and visual composition.
	Strong understanding of social media video formats and digital content trends.
	Ability to interpret briefs and transform ideas into engaging visual content.
	Strong attention to detail and creative problem-solving skills.&nbsp;


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: contact@feegor.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Video / Motion Animator Job at Feegor




        
    

        
        Feegor is an innovative B2B e-commerce company that enables retailers discover, connect, and source goods directly from manufacturers and major wholesalers nationwide. We are the technology backbone that ensures easy, trusted and efficient transactions in Nigeria’s Informal retail sector.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job osition: Video / Motion Animator
Job Location: Lagos

About the Role
     

            
    




	We are looking for a creative and detail-oriented Video / Motion Animator to join our team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for creating engaging visual content through animation, motion graphics, video editing, and storytelling to support our brand, marketing campaigns, product communication, and digital platforms.


Key Responsibilities


	Create engaging motion graphics, animations, and video content for marketing campaigns, social media, product launches, and internal communications.
	Develop creative concepts, storyboards, and visual narratives based on project requirements.
	Edit and enhance videos with strong attention to pacing, transitions, sound, and visual effects.
	Create 2D/3D animations, explainer videos, promotional videos, and branded content.
	Collaborate with designers, marketers, and other teams to translate ideas into compelling visual experiences.
	Maintain consistency with brand guidelines across all video and animation content.
	Stay updated on industry trends, animation styles, and emerging creative tools.
	Manage multiple projects while meeting deadlines and quality expectations.


Requirements


	Proven experience as a Video Animator, Motion Designer, or similar creative role.
	Strong portfolio showcasing motion graphics, animation, and video editing projects.
	Proficiency in tools such as Adobe After Effects, Premiere Pro, Illustrator, Photoshop, or similar software.
	Experience with animation principles, storytelling, typography, and visual composition.
	Strong understanding of social media video formats and digital content trends.
	Ability to interpret briefs and transform ideas into engaging visual content.
	Strong attention to detail and creative problem-solving skills. 


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Chi Farms Limited is Nigeria&rsquo;s leading and most integrated poultry company, providing top-quality products and services across the agricultural value chain. As part of the TGI Group, Chi Farms continues to expand its footprint in poultry, aquaculture, and frozen food distribution across Nigeria and West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location: KM51, Ogun

Job Description
Cost Control:


	Track daily operational costs across slaughtering, processing, packaging, and distribution with a view to ascertaining production cost per unit of output and order.
	Identify and investigate discrepancies between standard and actual costs (materials, labour, utilities) of production.
	Partner closely with the Engineering Team on monthly review of Energy and


Maintenance Costs:


	Partner closely with Warehouse Team on inventory reconciliation, stock write- off and other reporting matters.
	Prepare monthly cost reports, gross margin analysis, SKU profitability and landed cost calculations.
	Support the preparation of monthly forecast and annual budget for the business unit, aligning with production targets and sales forecasts.
	Recommend efficiency improvements in energy use, labour allocation, and supply chain management.


Fixed Asset Accounting:


	Manage sub-fixed asset register for the slaughterhouse.
	Partner with the Production and Engineering Team on CAPEX requirement, raising and seeking approval for CAPEX.
	Own the end-to-end process of asset capitalisation, depreciation and write-off/disposal/retirement considering all necessary control procedures and approvals.
	Coordinate external and internal visitors for asset verification and/or valuation exercises.


Skills and Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field; (ACA/ACCA/CIMA added advantage).
	Minimum of 5 years of proven experience preferable in an FMCG Company.
	Strong knowledge of manufacturing overheads, BOM (Bill of Materials), and product costing.
	Ability to interpret complex data and provide actionable insights.
	Familiarity with ERP systems (SAP and/or MS Nav 365), and Advance Ms Excel Skill.

Finance Manager Job at Chi Farms Limited




        
    

        
        Chi Farms Limited is Nigeria’s leading and most integrated poultry company, providing top-quality products and services across the agricultural value chain. As part of the TGI Group, Chi Farms continues to expand its footprint in poultry, aquaculture, and frozen food distribution across Nigeria and West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location: KM51, Ogun

Job Description
Cost Control:
     

            
    




	Track daily operational costs across slaughtering, processing, packaging, and distribution with a view to ascertaining production cost per unit of output and order.
	Identify and investigate discrepancies between standard and actual costs (materials, labour, utilities) of production.
	Partner closely with the Engineering Team on monthly review of Energy and


Maintenance Costs:


	Partner closely with Warehouse Team on inventory reconciliation, stock write- off and other reporting matters.
	Prepare monthly cost reports, gross margin analysis, SKU profitability and landed cost calculations.
	Support the preparation of monthly forecast and annual budget for the business unit, aligning with production targets and sales forecasts.
	Recommend efficiency improvements in energy use, labour allocation, and supply chain management.


Fixed Asset Accounting:


	Manage sub-fixed asset register for the slaughterhouse.
	Partner with the Production and Engineering Team on CAPEX requirement, raising and seeking approval for CAPEX.
	Own the end-to-end process of asset capitalisation, depreciation and write-off/disposal/retirement considering all necessary control procedures and approvals.
	Coordinate external and internal visitors for asset verification and/or valuation exercises.


Skills and Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field; (ACA/ACCA/CIMA added advantage).
	Minimum of 5 years of proven experience preferable in an FMCG Company.
	Strong knowledge of manufacturing overheads, BOM (Bill of Materials), and product costing.
	Ability to interpret complex data and provide actionable insights.
	Familiarity with ERP systems (SAP and/or MS Nav 365), and Advance Ms Excel Skill.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Tax / Treasury Specialist",
  "description": "Integrated Dairies Limited is a Nigeria-based dairy producer focused on delivering high-quality fresh milk and dairy products to consumers across Nigeria and the wider African market. Established in 2003, the company combines large-scale production with dairy-related research to improve product quality and innovation. Integrated Dairies is committed to advancing local dairy capabilities, supporting the agricultural value chain, and promoting food security. Team members join a growth-oriented environment that values operational excellence, responsible sourcing, and continuous improvement.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Tax / Treasury Specialist
Location: Vom, Plateau
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	The Tax and Treasury Specialist is responsible for managing the organization&rsquo;s fixed asset account, tax compliance, reporting, and planning, as well as overseeing treasury operations including cash management, liquidity forecasting, and banking relationships.
	This role ensures that all tax obligations are met accurately and on time while optimizing the company&rsquo;s cash flow and financial risk exposure.


Job Description
Tax Management:


	Statutory remittances; monthly computation and remittances of VAT, WHT, PAYE, NSITF, ITF (quarterly) and pension. Check the GLs to correctly reclassify the expense.
	Monitor changes in tax legislation and assess their impact on the organization.
	Assist in tax planning strategies to optimize the company&rsquo;s tax position.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date tax records and documentation.
	Prepare tax provisions, deferred tax calculations, and related financial disclosures.
	Enforces payment of outstanding taxes.
	Support tax audits and liaise with external tax advisors and tax authorities.


Treasury Management:


	Manage daily cash operations, including monitoring cash balances, funds transfers, and payment processing.
	Prepare short-term and long-term cash flow forecasts to support liquidity planning.
	Maintain and manage banking relationships, including account openings, closures, and signatory updates.
	Monitor foreign exchange exposure and assist in hedging strategies (e.g., forward contracts, swaps).
	Oversee and manage intercompany loans, borrowings, and investment activities.
	Ensure compliance with internal treasury policies, covenants, and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare treasury reports and analysis for management review.


Fixed Asset Management:


	Fixed Assets Register; maintain and update Fixed Assets Register (FAR), ensure all additions &amp; disposals are properly posted into GL Ac, ensure Fixed Assets Register align fully with GL, ensure items capitalised are within capitalisation policy and reconciliation of Capital WIP with GL and breakdown of balance.
	Intangible Assets and Right of use; ensure all additions are properly recorded, posted and agreed to GL and ensure correct capturing and posting of amortization
	Depreciation Schedule; ensure depreciation is in line with applicable accounting standards.
	Asset Verification and Control; Coordinate periodic physical verification of fixed assets, investigate and resolve discrepancies between physical and Fixed Assets Register (FAR) and ensure assets are properly tagged and tracked.
	Ensures compliance with accounting standards like International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP)
	Prepayment-Total card; Ensure weekly update and reconciliation of cards on ERP.
	Bank Account reconciliation; reconciliation of foreign bank accounts and loan accounts.
	Financial accounting; joining the team to review Management accounts and timely working on other financial tasks that may be assigned.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field.
	3 - 6 years of relevant experience in the role.
	Professional certifications such as ACCA, ICAN, or equivalent are an advantage.
	Proficiency in preparing Financial Statements and expertise in Financial Reporting
	A deep understanding of Finance principles.&nbsp;
	Professional certifications such as ACCA, ICAN, or equivalent are an advantage.
	Proficiency in accounting software (e.g., SAP, Oracle, QuickBooks) and treasury management systems (TMS).
	Familiarity with statutory remittance processes and compliance frameworks is preferred.
	Strong Analytical Skills, problem solving and organisation skills.
	Excellent Communication skills for collaborating with team members and stakeholders.
	Detail-oriented and proactive with the ability to meet deadlines.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@idlgroup.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-29",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-10",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

Tax / Treasury Specialist Job at Integrated Dairies Limited




        
    

        
        Integrated Dairies Limited is a Nigeria-based dairy producer focused on delivering high-quality fresh milk and dairy products to consumers across Nigeria and the wider African market. Established in 2003, the company combines large-scale production with dairy-related research to improve product quality and innovation. Integrated Dairies is committed to advancing local dairy capabilities, supporting the agricultural value chain, and promoting food security. Team members join a growth-oriented environment that values operational excellence, responsible sourcing, and continuous improvement.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Tax / Treasury Specialist
Location: Vom, Plateau
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	The Tax and Treasury Specialist is responsible for managing the organization’s fixed asset account, tax compliance, reporting, and planning, as well as overseeing treasury operations including cash management, liquidity forecasting, and banking relationships.
	This role ensures that all tax obligations are met accurately and on time while optimizing the company’s cash flow and financial risk exposure.


Job Description
Tax Management:


	Statutory remittances; monthly computation and remittances of VAT, WHT, PAYE, NSITF, ITF (quarterly) and pension. Check the GLs to correctly reclassify the expense.
	Monitor changes in tax legislation and assess their impact on the organization.
	Assist in tax planning strategies to optimize the company’s tax position.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date tax records and documentation.
	Prepare tax provisions, deferred tax calculations, and related financial disclosures.
	Enforces payment of outstanding taxes.
	Support tax audits and liaise with external tax advisors and tax authorities.


Treasury Management:


	Manage daily cash operations, including monitoring cash balances, funds transfers, and payment processing.
	Prepare short-term and long-term cash flow forecasts to support liquidity planning.
	Maintain and manage banking relationships, including account openings, closures, and signatory updates.
	Monitor foreign exchange exposure and assist in hedging strategies (e.g., forward contracts, swaps).
	Oversee and manage intercompany loans, borrowings, and investment activities.
	Ensure compliance with internal treasury policies, covenants, and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare treasury reports and analysis for management review.


Fixed Asset Management:


	Fixed Assets Register; maintain and update Fixed Assets Register (FAR), ensure all additions & disposals are properly posted into GL Ac, ensure Fixed Assets Register align fully with GL, ensure items capitalised are within capitalisation policy and reconciliation of Capital WIP with GL and breakdown of balance.
	Intangible Assets and Right of use; ensure all additions are properly recorded, posted and agreed to GL and ensure correct capturing and posting of amortization
	Depreciation Schedule; ensure depreciation is in line with applicable accounting standards.
	Asset Verification and Control; Coordinate periodic physical verification of fixed assets, investigate and resolve discrepancies between physical and Fixed Assets Register (FAR) and ensure assets are properly tagged and tracked.
	Ensures compliance with accounting standards like International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) or Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP)
	Prepayment-Total card; Ensure weekly update and reconciliation of cards on ERP.
	Bank Account reconciliation; reconciliation of foreign bank accounts and loan accounts.
	Financial accounting; joining the team to review Management accounts and timely working on other financial tasks that may be assigned.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field.
	3 - 6 years of relevant experience in the role.
	Professional certifications such as ACCA, ICAN, or equivalent are an advantage.
	Proficiency in preparing Financial Statements and expertise in Financial Reporting
	A deep understanding of Finance principles. 
	Professional certifications such as ACCA, ICAN, or equivalent are an advantage.
	Proficiency in accounting software (e.g., SAP, Oracle, QuickBooks) and treasury management systems (TMS).
	Familiarity with statutory remittance processes and compliance frameworks is preferred.
	Strong Analytical Skills, problem solving and organisation skills.
	Excellent Communication skills for collaborating with team members and stakeholders.
	Detail-oriented and proactive with the ability to meet deadlines.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
